key_insight: A hierarchical screening workflow can turn many machine-learning-predicted stable compounds into a smaller, experimentally actionable set of high-confidence synthesis targets.
engineering_relevance: This framework helps materials engineers prioritize candidates more realistically by filtering for thermodynamic and dynamical stability across increasingly rigorous first-principles checks.
method: Start from 894 reported stable materials and curate 603 unique structures.
Screen stability with complete PBE phase diagrams, then apply harmonic phonon and finite-temperature MD dynamical checks using machine-learning interatomic potentials.
Refine with SCAN phase diagrams and rank candidates by decomposition enthalpy and chemical-space completeness.
evidence_signal: From 603 unique structures, only 298 remained thermodynamically stable on full PBE phase diagrams, 166 passed dynamical screening, 109 survived SCAN refinement, and 25 were prioritized for synthesis.
watch_item: The workflow is computationally intensive and depends on the accuracy of phase diagrams, phonons, and ML potentials, so false positives or missed candidates remain possible.
paper_title: Predicting Novel Stable Materials for Experimental Synthesis
